source_evidence: Eq. (1): min_a V = l^T a  s.t. B^T n = f, -sigma_C a_i <= n_i <= sigma_T a_i, i=1..N_b. 'The minimum volume truss that satisfies the force equilibrium equations (plastic analysis)'.
purpose: Finds the minimum-volume truss (member cross-sectional areas) that satisfies nodal force equilibrium subject to member stress limits in tension and compression, over a redundant ground structure.
assumptions: Plastic analysis: only force equilibrium enforced, compatibility and stress-strain relations not included
Redundancy N_b - N_dof must be greater than zero
formula_or_algorithm: min over a of V = l^T a subject to B^T n = f and -sigma_C a_i <= n_i <= sigma_T a_i for i = 1..N_b. B^T is the N_dof x N_b nodal equilibrium matrix built from member directional cosines; n is the vector of member axial forces. A member is absent (removed) if a_i = 0. This is a nonlinear program.
validation_opportunities: benchmark against known optimal volumes (torsion cylinder, cone, ball)
dimensional check on V = length * area

reuse_risk.explanation: Formulation is general and well-defined, but correct assembly of B^T from geometry and satisfying redundancy/stability conditions requires care; it is a nonlinear program, less efficient to solve than its LP reformulation.

missing_information: Concrete assembly procedure for B^T from directional cosines and connectivity
Handling of near-zero areas for member removal
